details: Spectral Data section shows '2-Coumaranone: mp 49°; IR, cm-1: 1808, 1832' — matches compound, value, property, and source paper exactly.
Table 8 row 16 contains '2,2-dimethyl-1-propanol 113.10' as the experimental Bp column; review-paper compilation of experimental Bp values is correctly labeled measured.
Paper states 'its relatively high melting point (28 °C)' for sulfolane in discussion of PS surface chemistry; compound, value, property, and source match.
Paper reports Tm = 354.11 K for fenofibrate in multiple places; K-to-°C conversion 354.11 − 273.15 = 80.96 is arithmetically correct.
Analytical and Spectral Data: '1-(1-Pentafluorophenyl-1H-pyrrole-2-yl)-ethanone (2b): 0.83 g (70% yield); m.p: 45-48 oC' — full match.
Section for compound 2k states '5-Acetyl-4-(4-chlorophenylamino)-2-ethyl-6-phenylpyridazin-3(2H)-one, 2k Yield = 64%; mp = 183–184 °C (EtOH)' — exact match.

Paper section 3.24 reports '1-(4-Benzhydryl-3-(benzo[d][1,3]dioxol-5-yl)isoquinolin-2(1H)-yl)ethan-1-one (4ca) ... mp 183.1–250.2 °C (CHCl 3)' verbatim; the unusually wide range is what the paper actually reports, so the extraction is faithful to the source.
Experimental section: 'Base 9a was obtained in 74% yield, m.p. 97-99°C (acetone)' for 2-[3-(4-phenyl-1-piperazinyl)propyl]-1-phenyl-1,2-dihydropyridazine-3,6-dione — full match.
Section on TCMTB lists 'boiling point: >120 °C, melting point: <−10 °C, relative density (water = 1): 1.4'; relation '<' correctly captured.
Section 4.2.13 states '6,8-Dibromoquinolin-2(1H)-one (2m). A white solid; mp = 226−228 °C' — exact match.

Table 1 header is 'Tpeak of the salt melting (°C)' and row 6 (diMeMEA diclofenac) shows 111. The 'Tpeak of melting' label refers to the DSC melting peak; classifying as melting_point is acceptable since the column explicitly reports melting (not decomposition); compound and value match.
Table 1 lists 'methylbutane 113.3' under C5H12 melting points; K→°C conversion 113.3 − 273.15 = -159.85 is arithmetically correct.
Table 1 lists 'methylcyclohexane 146.6' under C7H14 melting points; K→°C conversion 146.6 − 273.15 = -126.55 is arithmetically correct.

Compound 6e entry: '4-((Methyl((1R,2R,3R,5S)-2,6,6-trimethylbicyclo[3.1.1]heptan-3-yl)amino)methyl)-N-(4-methyl-3-((4-(pyridin-3-yl)pyrimidin-2-yl)amino)phenyl)benzamide 6e Yield 91%. mp 118–120 °C' — exact match including stereo descriptors.
Section 4.1.5 reports compound 12e as 'Brown solid; yield: 71%; MP = 251–253 °C' for the named coumarin-bis-triazole hybrid — full match.
Section 3.2 reports 'sharp endothermic peak at 198 °C ... indicated the melting of the new phase' for the ITR-TER cocrystal; correctly attributed to the cocrystal melting point.
Synthesis paragraph for compound 1a reports 'Na-formyl-16β-hydroxyaspidospermidine (1a) (68.5 mg, 98 %) as colouress plates, m.p. 66-68 °C' — full match.

Compound 5l entry: '2-((2-Fluorobenzyl)thio)-5-(1-methyl-3-(trifluoromethyl)-1H-pyrazol-4-yl)-1,3,4-oxadiazole (5l) M.p. 101-101 °C' — exact match; the 101-101 is the paper's own (likely typo) text, and the extractor faithfully recorded it.
Section 3.2.1 reports '(Sp)-3,3′-(1,4(1,4)-dibenzenacyclohexaphane-12,43-diyl)diphenol [(Sp)-2b]. White solid (998 mg, 85% yield); M.p. 214–215 °C' — exact match.
Compound 4d entry: '1,1'-(Pentane-1,5-diyl)bis{3-[(3,5-dimethyladamantan-1-yl)methyl]urea} (4d) was prepared ... Yield 0.188 g (76%), mp 127.1°C'; Table 1 also lists 127.1. Full match.
Table 2 row 2 names '(R)-2-methyl-5-(5-methyl-3-(p-tolyl)-4,5-dihydroisoxazol-5-yl)cyclohex-2-en-1-one' (compound 2) with 'm.p: 96-98 °C' in the analytical-data column — full match.
Compound 1h section: '2′-bromo-4-nitro-[1,1′-biphenyl]-2-carbaldehyde (1h). Yield: 78%; Mp: 85 °C' — exact match.

Table 1 row d reports '66-67(m.p.) 85' (yield 85%) for product 3d, which is identified in the experimental section as '2,8.8-trimethyl-3,3-dichlorotricyclo[4.1.1.0^{2,4}]octane (3d)'. Compound, value, property, source all match.
Table 5 columns are Enthalpy/Onset/Peak/Crystallization-Onset; Methanoic acid row shows Peak = -49.4 ± 0.01 °C. property=DSC_peak is correct; value, units, units conversion, meas_calc all correct.
Section 3.7.10 reports for compound 8j 'shiny yellow microcrystals with 32% yield with mp 155–156 °C' — full match with the named glucose-triazole derivative.
